semantic.role: Type contract layer defining the core abstractions for LLM interaction: transport, provider, client, and response contracts. Serves as the canonical type specification that all LLM providers must implement.
responsibilities: Define UsageInfo for tracking LLM token consumption (input, output, total)
Define LLMResponse as the generic structure for LLM responses, including JSON and markdown variants and optional usage info
Define LLMClient as the primary interface for making LLM requests and receiving responses
Define PreviousSemanticContext for passing historical semantic drift analysis into LLM prompts
Define LLMProvider as the interface that each concrete provider (OpenAI, Gemini, etc.) must implement
Define ProviderConfig for provider-specific configuration parameters
Define LLMTransport as the abstract transport layer for communication with LLM APIs
outOfScope: Concrete implementations of specific LLM providers (e.g., OpenAI, Gemini, Mock)
Configuration loading, environment variable parsing, or secrets management
Retry logic, error handling, or timeout management for LLM calls
Prompt building, template rendering, or context aggregation
Token counting or cost estimation logic
HTTP client setup or connection pooling
invariants: All LLM providers must implement the LLMProvider interface defined here
The LLMClient interface is the single entry point for making LLM calls across the system
LLMResponse must always include a json field (any type) and a markdown Record<string, string>

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Establish a stable, type-safe contract layer for all LLM integrations to decouple providers from consumers and enable provider swapping without affecting higher-level services.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Not applicable – no changes detected in the provided AST or header.

publicSurface.description: Records token consumption for an LLM request, including inputTokens, outputTokens, and totalTokens.
Standard response shape from an LLM call containing a generic JSON payload, markdown key-value map, and optional usage statistics.
Primary interface for executing LLM requests. All system components that need LLM capabilities depend on this contract.
Stores the previous semantic analysis context for drift detection, enabling the system to compare past and present module contracts.
Interface that each concrete LLM provider (e.g., OpenAI, Gemini) must implement to integrate with ArchSpine.
Configuration contract for initializing and customizing an LLM provider instance.
Abstract transport contract for low-level communication with LLM APIs, handling HTTP or other protocols.
